LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT OSCAR ZAVALETA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE SEPTEMBER 12, 2017 ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS GRANTED. MR. ZAVALETA HAS SHOWN THAT HE IS ENTITLED TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT PER CCP 473(B) BASED ON SURPRISE AND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. GRANTING THE MOTION IS IN ACCORD WITH CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS AND PLAINTIFF JOSE GUEVARA HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HE WOULD SUFFER ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE BY THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. MR. ZAVALETA'S REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. PUNITIVE DAMAGES CANNOT BE AWARDED ON A MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG

Attorneys, Edward M. Higginbotham and Conrad K. Wu of the law offices of Edward M. Higginbotham (415-581-8885) appear as counsels for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Victor M. Marques of the law offices of Marquez Law Group (415-314-7831) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court admonishes counsel that conduct in this litigation was inconsistent with set of rules of civility and long-standing California law.

The court re-affirms its prior tentative ruling: Defendant Oscar Zavaleta's motion to set aside the September 12, 2017 entry of default is granted. Mr. Zavaleta has shown that he is entitled to set aside the default per CCP 473(b) based on surprise and excusable neglect. Granting the motion is in accord with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff Jose Guevara has not shown that he would suffer any legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. Mr. Zavaleta's request for punitive damages is denied. Punitive damages cannot be awarded on a motion to set aside default. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

**Judge Kahn's tentative ruling - Defendant Oscar Zavaleta's motion to set aside entry of default is granted. Mr. Zavaleta has shown that he is entitled to set aside the default per CCP 473(b) based on surprise and excusable neglect. Granting the motion is in accord with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff Jose Guevara has not shown that he would suffer any legal prejudice by the granting of the motion. Mr. Zavaleta's request for punitive damages is denied. Punitive damages cannot be awarded on a motion to set aside default. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Zavaleta is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling is contested, the hearing will be on October 30, 2017, not October 27, 2017 due to the unavailability of Judge Kahn on October 27.**
